message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: McDonald's (Smoke Tree Commons) (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 118 reviews at 2465 E Palm Canyon Dr; Tripadvisor ~30 reviews; Google reported ~885 reviewers via aggregator (3.8 stars) [UNVERIFIED on Google direct]
- Sentiment: mixed (Tripadvisor 3.3/5; common complaints about order accuracy, broken ice cream machine, service inconsistency; some positive notes about staff and cleanliness)
- Longevity: [UNVERIFIED] specific opening year for this location; Smoke Tree Commons shopping center is an established south Palm Springs retail node
- Credentials: ["Listed by Visit Palm Springs (official tourism bureau) as a notable mid-century-modern designed McDonald's"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for 'unique McDonald's architecture Palm Springs', 'mid-century modern McDonald's', and 'McDonald's Smoke Tree Commons'. Does NOT surface for 'best restaurants Coachella Valley', 'best breakfast Palm Springs', or general dining queries — appears only in fast-food list aggregations (Yelp, Tripadvisor 'Quick Bites' rankings, where it sits near the bottom: #38 of 42 Quick Bites in Palm Springs).
- Site structured data: McDonald's corporate location page (mcdonalds.com/.../32041.html) is the canonical site — likely carries Restaurant schema.org markup typical of corporate McDonald's pages, with address/hours/services. No independent site exists for this specific location; no special microsite acknowledges the architectural significance.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about McDonald's Smoke Tree Commons in Palm Springs:

## Location and Design

McDonald's is a uniquely designed midcentury modern fast-food restaurant located in the southern part of Palm Springs. The stylish architecture blends seamlessly with the city's iconic desert-modern aesthetic, making it a local landmark for both visitors and residents.

## Smoke Tree Commons Shopping Center

Smoke Tree Commons, constructed in 2008, features Desert Modern architecture and serves as a significant shopping destination. It is anchored by Jensen's Gourmet Grocery, Michaels, Cost Plus World Market, TJ Maxx, Petco, Big 5 Sporting Goods, and Walgreens. Other tenants include McDonald's, Chipotle, and Chase Bank.

## Visitor Experience

This vibrant shopping center has a great mix of businesses all done in the context of a really pleasant and inviting outdoor setting, with permanent tent style awnings that line the walkway between World Market and Jensen's.

The McDonald's location specifically benefits from its placement within this thoughtfully designed shopping center that respects Palm Springs' architectural heritage with its mid-century modern aesthetic.

Here are the reviews for McDonald's at 2465 E Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, CA:

## Overall Rating

The McDonald's has a 3.8 Star Rating from 885 reviewers.

## Positive Reviews

One customer praised the location, noting excellent customer service with fabulous staff and food that was not that bad. The restaurant offers a friendly atmosphere with a retro vibe playing old TV shows, with staff including the crew lead being exceptionally friendly and professional.

Some customers reported that this McDonald's has really improved lately, with fresh and delicious breakfast items like Sausage Biscuit and scrambled eggs, especially when ordered early in the morning.

## Negative Reviews

One reviewer gave a scathing review about the online phone ordering system not working properly and dismissive staff, with complaints about broken kiosks requiring excessive screen-banging.

Some experiences with food quality vary, with reports of soggy fries and stale nuggets, and dissatisfaction with Big Mac sauce being too much. Another review mentioned that fish and chicken sandwiches appeared poorly assembled, and that the food tasted burnt due to oil needing to be changed in the fryers.

## General Observations

The restaurant is clean with generally good food, though prices are a bit high, and seating may be limited during busy hours, but service remains cheerful and helpful.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fast food options in Palm Springs and the Coachella Valley:

## Top-Rated Fast Food Chains

Popular options include In-N-Out Burger, Raising Cane's Chicken Fingers, McDonald's, Panda Express, Jack in the Box, Blaze Pizza, Chipotle Mexican Grill, Del Taco, and Chick-fil-A.

## Standout Recommendations

**Raising Cane's Chicken Fingers**: Located on Ramon Road near Gene Autry in Palm Springs, their chicken fingers are highly praised, with recommendations to try the Box Combo featuring 4 chicken fingers, fries, Texas toast, coleslaw, and a fountain drink.

**Del Taco**: This Del Taco location in the Coachella Valley is known for being one of the best in the area. Del Taco offers Mexican cuisine such as tacos, burritos, and quesadillas, and also serves breakfast.

**Five Guys**: You can enjoy juicy burgers from Five Guys.

**McDonald's**: The McDonald's inside the Walmart in Palm Springs is very well maintained and clean throughout the day.

The area offers diverse fast food options catering to various tastes and preferences, from classic burgers to Mexican cuisine.

Based on the web page content provided, there are **no listing details available for McDonald's Smoke Tree Commons**. 

The page appears to be a navigation and footer section from the Visit Palm Springs tourism website. While the page title mentions "McDonald's Smoke Tree Commons," the actual content body contains only navigation menus, contact information, social media links, and footer elements—no descriptive information about this location, its architecture, features, or categories.

To find details about this property, you would need to access the full listing page rather than this navigation/footer section.

# McDonald's Palm Springs - Rating & Reviews Summary

**Rating & Ranking:**
- Rating: 3.3 out of 5 stars
- Reviews: 30 total
- Ranking: #38 of 42 Quick Bites in Palm Springs

**Common Praises:**
Customers highlighted the restaurant's cleanliness and modern appearance. One reviewer noted it was "one of the best McDonald's" they'd visited globally, praising the "Wonderful customer service and a very clean establishment." The outdoor seating in the shopping center location and friendly evening staff were also frequently mentioned positively.

**Common Complaints:**
Service inconsistencies emerged as a major issue. Customers reported missing items from orders, with one noting items were "not included" fairly often. Drive-through wait times were problematic—one reviewer described being "stuck" for over 30 minutes. Additionally, multiple complaints involved the ice cream machine being "broken or being cleaned frequently," with staff appearing indifferent to customer disappointment about unavailable items.

**Popular Breakfast Spots:**

Cheeky's is generally a long line to get in and is closed on Tuesdays, but it's known for its world-famous bacon flight, Mimosas, Custard style cheesy eggs, and waffles.

Cheeky's serves everything made from scratch using the freshest local ingredients, with creative meditations on breakfast and lunch in a bright indoor-outdoor setting.

Broken Yolk Cafe delivers a mix of both classic and creative breakfast, brunch, and lunch offerings and is a favorite for locals and visitors alike—praised for its generous portions, reliable quality, and warm, casual vibe.

**Other Notable Options:**

- L'Atelier is a French café with delicious quiche and excellent iced coffee

contradicts: Claim that Visit Palm Springs lists it 'as a notable mid-century-modern designed McDonald's' — when the actual Visit Palm Springs listing page was fetched, the body contained no descriptive text about architecture, mid-century modern design, or architectural significance. The listing exists in the directory, but the 'mid-century modern designed McDonald's' framing appears to come from search-snippet summarization rather than the listing itself. The credential as stated overreaches what the official tourism page actually says.

Claim that the entity 'surfaces strongly for unique McDonald\'s architecture Palm Springs / mid-century modern McDonald\'s' — searches return the Visit Palm Springs directory listing and a couple of Instagram posts, but no substantial editorial or architectural-press coverage (no Palm Springs Modernism Week, PS Preservation Foundation, Curbed, Dezeen, etc. results). 'Surfaces strongly' overstates a thin presence.
flagged_as_unverified: Specific opening year for the Smoke Tree Commons McDonald's (shopping center reportedly built 2008, but McDonald's opening year not independently confirmed)
Google direct review count of 885 / 3.8 stars (only surfaced via aggregator snippet, not verified on Google Maps directly)
Schema.org / JSON-LD Restaurant markup on the McDonald's corporate location page — WebFetch on the corporate URL timed out, so structured-data claim could not be independently confirmed

Whether the building is genuinely architecturally significant vs. simply being a standard McDonald's inside a mid-century-styled 2008 shopping center (the latter is more consistent with available evidence — Smoke Tree Commons itself was built 2008, so this is not a historic mid-century structure)
input.notes: Review-volume and sentiment claims hold up well. The architectural-credential claim is the weakest link: the Visit Palm Springs listing exists but doesn't contain the architectural language attributed to it, and Smoke Tree Commons was built in 2008 — so this is desert-modern-styled retail, not authentic mid-century heritage. Visibility-gap score of 6 and anchor-reference tier feel roughly right given thin editorial coverage and complete absence from best-of dining lists. Recommend softening the architectural framing in the brief.
